posts.title: Hypertrophy: What Meta-Analyses Usually Agree On
posts.excerpt: Volume, proximity to failure, frequency—here’s the overlap across reviews and how to apply it without overthinking.
posts.content:
# Hypertrophy: what meta-analyses usually agree on

There’s a lot of noise in fitness content. This post focuses on the overlap you’ll see across many reviews:

## 1) Weekly hard sets matter
If you train a muscle consistently and progressively, you’ll grow. Start with a realistic weekly set target and adjust based on recovery and progress.

## 2) Get close enough to failure
Most sets should be challenging. You don’t need to fail every set, but **easy sets rarely build much size**.

## 3) Frequency helps practicality
Hitting a muscle *at least* twice per week often makes volume easier to distribute and recovery easier to manage.

## Simple takeaway
Pick a split you can repeat for months. Track performance. Add reps or weight over time. Eat enough protein. Sleep.

> Tip: write your plan like a checklist. Consistency beats novelty.
